headline: Allstate Wiretap Lawsuit Puts Data Privacy And Telematics Strategy Under Review
summary: Allstate is facing a class action lawsuit alleging it monitored drivers through cellphone data without proper consent. Plaintiffs claim the insurer used this data to adjust premiums, deny coverage, and sell information to other insurers. A U.S. District Judge has allowed the case to proceed under the Federal Wiretap Act, increasing legal and regulatory scrutiny. For investors watching NYSE:ALL, this case puts a spotlight on how a large auto and home insurer handles customer data at a time...
